tweets.text:
Space ETFs are exploding in popularity:

Total assets under management (AUM) in space-themed ETFs has surpassed $5 billion for the first time in history.

Since the start of 2026, space ETF AUM has more than DOUBLED.

Over the last year, this figure has surged by over +900%.

Meanwhile, the Space Innovators ETF, $NASA, launched on March 30th, has grown its AUM to a record $2.6 billion.

Over this period, the fund price has surged +50%, including a +37% increase in May alone.

This comes as $NASA is one of the few investment vehicles available to retail investors that offers exposure to SpaceX, which currently accounts for 7.5% of the fund.

Retail is ready for the SpaceX IPO.

tweets.text:
World Labs CEO Dr. Fei-Fei Li: "The world is not made of words."

"Language models have given machines an extraordinary command of concepts, vocabulary, and reasoning, but the physical world, virtual or real, runs on a different substrate."

"Where language models learn the statistical structure of text, world models learn the statistical structure of space and time: how light falls on a surface, how a garden looks from an angle no camera has captured, how objects respond to force and follow the laws of physics."

"Language gave machines a way to talk about that world. World models are how machines will finally come to understand, imagine, reason and interact with it."

Full piece: https://t.co/C9qOJg5wuc

tweets.text:
India's oil product exports are collapsing as the Iran War forces refiners to keep fuel at home:

India's oil product exports dropped -31% YoY in May, to 878,000 barrels per day, the lowest since October 2022.

This also marks the 3rd consecutive monthly decline, totaling ~270,000 barrels.

By comparison, the 2025 high was ~1,500,000 barrels per day in September, or +70% larger.

This comes as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz has severely restricted crude and fuel flows to India, prompting refiners to redirect output toward domestic supply rather than overseas markets.

Indian refiners have also shifted production toward LPG at the expense of other exportable fuels, further weighing on overall outbound shipments.

Before the Iran War, the country imported ~90% of its LPG supply from the Middle East, making domestic production a strategic necessity once Hormuz flows were disrupted.

Domestic energy security is now a top priority in India.
tweets.full_text:
India's oil product exports are collapsing as the Iran War forces refiners to keep fuel at home:

India's oil product exports dropped -31% YoY in May, to 878,000 barrels per day, the lowest since October 2022.

This also marks the 3rd consecutive monthly decline, totaling ~270,000 barrels.

By comparison, the 2025 high was ~1,500,000 barrels per day in September, or +70% larger.

This comes as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz has severely restricted crude and fuel flows to India, prompting refiners to redirect output toward domestic supply rather than overseas markets.

Indian refiners have also shifted production toward LPG at the expense of other exportable fuels, further weighing on overall outbound shipments.

Before the Iran War, the country imported ~90% of its LPG supply from the Middle East, making domestic production a strategic necessity once Hormuz flows were disrupted.

Domestic energy security is now a top priority in India.

tweets.quoted_tweet_text: The more an asset matures, the more its susceptible to cross-asset risks. I'm not surprised to see Bitcoin pulling back here amidst macro concerns. We've yet to see a meaningful period in which Bitcoin isn't correlated with measures of risk like credit spreads (inverted in https://t.co/6QxQhR6agH

thread_tweets.text:
That's a fair adjustment. On the other hand, the extreme winners are more contained to one small segment this cycle. Same stat for R3000 was 210 stocks in 2000 and 218 in 2021...only 43 today.

We also run a bubble screen at the index and industry level. No index qualifies currently. Only two industries (semis + construction/engineering) qualify at the industry level. In comparison, 8 separate industries qualified at the 2000 top. Also, seeing multiples contract this year, which is abnormal for a blowoff top/bubble end.

tweets.text:
"Most assume local currency stablecoins are a worse store of value than USD. @nlevine19 on @TokenizedPod says the opposite can be true: 

"There are certain markets where the inflation is five, six, seven percent, but the yield that you get on underlying treasuries is like fifteen percent."

"When people think about holding local currency stablecoins, they're worried that 'This is gonna devalue significantly. I'm gonna lose a lot of money.' But if you're able to actually capture the treasury yield and pass it on, you could actually end up with a more stable or better product than holding US dollars themselves."

@blauyourmind @cuysheffield @gmoneyNFT

tweets.text:
Gold is sitting right at its 200-day moving average.

Yes, the last time we were here turned out to be a great buying opportunity…. but I try not to get too fixated on technical levels alone.

What stands out to me is how dramatically sentiment has shifted.

Just a few months ago, gold was one of the market’s favorite trades.

Today, it feels almost completely forgotten.

Not to steal from Buffett, but I'm starting to get greedy while others are becoming fearful.

https://t.co/P4sfxoqBM9

thread_tweets.text:
After Friday's jobs report, remember: 

There are 135 million private sector jobs.   

25% of the labor market is recession-proof.  

Another 59% barely moves.  

Only 15.5% actually drives the cycle.  

This chart shows the full breakdown, ranked by cyclicality: https://t.co/X3lPamAB44

tweets.text:
I've followed @NEARProtocol  more closely than any other L1 over the past two years. I've spoken with Illia on several occasions.

Most people miss this: it didn't start as a blockchain. It started as an AI company. The inability to pay globally distributed developers forced its founders to build the protocol.

Blockchain was the means. It was always about building toward the inevitable. The agentic era.

What's striking now is watching those deliberate design decisions — made from the outset — click into place. NEAR didn't pivot to AI. It was AI first. 

And as we know, the best tech doesn't always win in crypto. Product-market fit does.  Next week on @RealVision, @ilblackdragon returns, joined by @sal_ternullo from @SVRN.
